brief_document.title: VisibilityStore trait, WorkflowSummary, and ListWorkflowsFilter
brief_document.purpose: Define the VisibilityStore trait abstracting the search index, the WorkflowSummary projection type returned by queries, and the ListWorkflowsFilter for filtered workflow listing. These types define the query contract that backend implementations fulfill.
brief_document.task: Add to aion-store: VisibilityStore trait with record_visibility (upsert a workflow's visibility record), list_workflows (filtered query returning WorkflowSummary), and count_workflows (filtered count). Define WorkflowSummary with workflow_id, run_id, workflow_type, status, start_time, close_time, and search_attributes. Define ListWorkflowsFilter with optional predicates for workflow_type, status, time range, and custom attribute value comparisons.

requirements.title: VisibilityStore trait
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define VisibilityStore as an async trait in aion-store with methods: record_visibility(record: VisibilityRecord), list_workflows(filter: ListWorkflowsFilter) -> Vec<WorkflowSummary>, count_workflows(filter: ListWorkflowsFilter) -> u64.
acceptance: VisibilityStore trait exists in aion-store with the three specified methods.
The trait is object-safe (can be used as Arc<dyn VisibilityStore>).
VisibilityRecord carries all fields needed to upsert a workflow's visibility entry.

requirements.title: WorkflowSummary projection type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WorkflowSummary with fields: workflow_id (WorkflowId), run_id (RunId), workflow_type (String), status (WorkflowStatus), start_time (DateTime<Utc>), close_time (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), and search_attributes (HashMap<String, SearchAttributeValue>).
acceptance: WorkflowSummary exists with all specified fields.
WorkflowSummary derives Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize.
WorkflowSummary does NOT contain the full event history.

requirements.title: ListWorkflowsFilter with typed predicates
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define ListWorkflowsFilter with optional fields: workflow_type (Option<String>), status (Option<WorkflowStatus>), started_after (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), started_before (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), closed_after (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), closed_before (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), search_attributes (Vec<SearchAttributePredicate>), limit (Option<u32>), offset (Option<u32>).
acceptance: ListWorkflowsFilter exists with all specified fields.
SearchAttributePredicate supports equality, greater-than, less-than, and contains for KeywordList.
An empty filter matches all workflows.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a backend (libSQL or otherwise) — that is AV-003.
SHALL NOT implement engine integration — that is AV-004.
SHALL NOT add server endpoints — that is AV-005.

constraints.text: VisibilityStore is a trait in aion-store, not coupled to any specific backend.
The libSQL implementation must support filtering by all standard fields and custom attribute predicates without full table scans.
Search attribute types are registered per namespace; type mismatches are errors, not silent coercions.
Visibility updates must not block the EventStore append path in server mode.
WorkflowSummary must include enough data for dashboard rendering without per-workflow history reads.
The search attribute event (SearchAttributesUpdated) is a recorded event subject to replay and determinism — it must be replayable.

resolved_context.intention: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running workflows of type Y. Without a query layer, the only access path is by WorkflowId — which requires knowing the ID up front. This cluster adds search attributes (user-defined key-value metadata on workflows) and a visibility index that supports filtered listing, full-text-like search on attributes, and time-range queries. This is the operational backbone that makes dashboards, alerting, and debugging possible at scale.

provenance.quote: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running
